subject: Computer Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 8: DATABASES
topics: Databases are organised collections of data that allow for efficient storage, retrieval, and management of
information. This topic introduces Structured Query Language (SQL), the universal standard for interacting
with relational databases. Learners will move from understanding basic data organisation to designing
schemas and writing SQL scripts to create and modify database structures.
activities: Design and create databases using SQL.
Classroom arranged for group work and pair discussions
Space to lay out index cards for the physical database simulation
Computers with internet access to run SQL in an online sandbox (SQLiteOnline.com or
resources: Code.org. (2024). CS fundamentals. Retrieved from https://code.org/educate/csf

assessment: Scenario: A small video club in Matero, Lusaka, wants to move from a paper list to a computer
database. They have one notebook where they write the customer’s name, the movie they rented,
and the date. They notice that if “Banda” rents a movie three times, his name is written over and over
(Schema Design) Why is this “one notebook” method inefficient? Draw a quick sketch showing
how you could split this information into two tables (Customers and Rentals) to avoid repeating
